channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Blu-ray is dying so I believe it's now time for 'obsolete' tape to shine again in the strangest of ironies
item.description: Sony announced that it is shutting down Blu-ray media production forever, so is tape the alternative?
item.dc:creator: desire.athow@futurenet.com (Desire Athow), TechRadar

item.title: New federal CIO onboard at OMB
item.description: Greg Barbaccia takes over for Clare Martorana as the government’s lead technology executive. The post New federal CIO onboard at OMB first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: People are hawking TikTok-loaded phones for thousands on eBay, Facebook
item.description: People are listing phones preloaded with TikTok for tens of thousands of dollars on eBay, Facebook marketplace and other online storefronts — though it is not clear if there are many buyers at those prices. TikTok was briefly unavailable to U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ubisoft reveals Assassin's Creed Shadows PC requirements – and just as I thought, you're safe with your RTX 4000 series GPU
item.description: Nvidia's new RTX 5000 GPU lineup promises to enhance gaming performance in multiple games, but PC requirements for Ubisoft's new title suggest an upgrade isn't urgently needed.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google pledges to crack down on fake reviews after UK watchdog investigation
item.description: Google is pledging to crack down on fake online reviews with tougher punishments for rogue reviewers and businesses after an investigation by regulators in Britain.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Watch 8: latest rumors, updates, and what we want to see
item.description: Samsung is thought to be working on another smartwatch for 2025 – here's what we think we know so far.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: El Hospital Sant Joan de Déu mejora la atención al paciente a través de un nuevo modelo de gestión de datos
item.description: El Hospital Sant Joan de Déu ha recurrido a la tecnología de Denodo para gestionar, explotar y analizar de manera rápida, efectiva y de forma ética los datos del centro. El propósito no es otro que dar un paso adelante en la mejora de la atención y el diagnóstico de sus pacientes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The first Android 16 public beta is here – and it borrows a key iOS feature
item.description: The first beta version of Android 16 is now available for Pixel phones, and there's one big new feature.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: El 29% de los CDO no ven futuro en el puesto
item.description: Casi un tercio de los líderes de TI que ocupan el puesto de director de datos y funciones similares en empresas de la lista Fortune 1000 no creen en el futuro a largo plazo del puesto de CDO.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A new Instagram feature might expose your embarrassing habits
item.description: Instagram Reels has added a new feature that shows you a feed of videos that your friends have liked. The bad news: It works both ways, meaning your friends can now see every video you’ve liked.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: AMD’s unpatched chip microcode glitch may require extreme measures by CISOs
item.description: Due to a partner disclosure, AMD has found itself in the unenviable position of having to confirm the existence of a major cybersecurity problem in its chip microcode before it could post a fix. But that headache is mild compared to those of their customers’ CISOs.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: TikTok France is being sued by 7 families. Here’s why
item.description: In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Over 60M kids' data may have been stolen: What you need to know
item.description: More than 62 million students and 9.5 million teachers may have had their data stolen, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Palo Alto Networks releases QRNG API framework
item.description: Cybersecurity company Palo Alto Networks has released the Quantum Random Number Generator (QRNG) Open API framework, enabling users to prepare for quantum security threats.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Seeking growth, UK competition regulator probes Apple and Google
item.description: Apple and Google now face regulatory probes in the ailing UK market, where the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has confirmed it intends to investigate both company’s mobile ecosystems under new digital market laws.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Wilson And Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: OpenAI opposes data deletion demand in India citing US legal constraints
item.description: OpenAI has informed the Delhi High Court that any directive requiring it to delete training data used for ChatGPT would conflict with its legal obligations under US law.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to delete Facebook, Instagram and Threads if you don't like Meta's changes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Should I stay or should I go — from Meta's social media platforms? That's what some Facebook, Instagram and Threads users are wondering after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's announcement this month that the company is relaxing rules on harmful content such as hate speech and abandoning its
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: This flying motorcycle can take you from traffic to sky in minutes
item.description: The Skyrider X1 from Rictor claims to be the "first amphibious flying passenger motorcycle," and it combines land and air travel in one sleek design.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taiwan plans to cull up to 120,000 green iguanas due to their negative impact on agriculture
item.description: Taiwan plans to cull up to 120,000 green iguanas, with supporters urging humane methods to bring down the animal's population, which is wreaking havoc on the island's agricultural sector.
item.dc:creator: Tai-Jing Wu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Stratoshark analyzes cloud applications at a syscall level
item.description: Securing cloud applications is both easy and hard. Easy because hyperscale providers such as Microsoft Azure are focused on ensuring that tenant compute environments are locked down and isolated from each other.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI at Work: How SOS 102 is Building Safer Communities in Kazakhstan
item.description: Emergency response systems worldwide are undergoing rapid transformation, and Kazakhstan is no exception. With the introduction of the SOS 102 app, the country has set a new standard for public safety, redefining how law enforcement interacts with citizens.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Emergency Response Transformed: Inside Kazakhstan’s SOS 102 System
item.description: Emergency response systems worldwide are undergoing rapid transformation, and Kazakhstan is no exception. With the introduction of the SOS 102 app, the country has set a new standard for public safety, redefining how law enforcement interacts with citizens.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk clashes with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over Trump-supported Stargate AI data center project
item.description: Elon Musk is clashing with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over the Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project touted by President Donald Trump, the latest in a feud between the two tech billionaires that started on OpenAI's board and is now testing Musk's influence with the new president.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn't in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star. Donaldson stirred interest in a Jan. 13 post on X saying he’d “buy TikTok so it doesn’t get banned.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Stratoshark brings Wireshark-style analysis to cloud system calls
item.description: The open-source Wireshark network protocol analyzer has been a standard tool for networking professionals for decades. In recent years, organizations have increasingly moved workloads to the cloud, where they have not had the same network visibility.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of setting fire to congressman's office over TikTok ban charged with arson
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Prosecutors charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman's office because he was upset with the federal TikTok ban with multiple counts on Wednesday, including arson.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Tuesday that he had pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 10 tech upgrades to save your time, privacy and money this year
item.description: Today's tech can help make life so much easier — if you know how to use it. Here are a few tips on the quickest and best ways to make your phone, laptop and other devices work for you.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: From devops to CTO: 5 things to start doing now
item.description: I was promoted to CTO in my late twenties, and while it is common to see young CTOs leading startups these days, it was unusual in the ’90s. I was far less experienced back then, and still developing my business acumen.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to stop robocalls on your flip phone
item.description: Flip phones are harder to block spam calls from, but it's not impossible. Kurt the "CyberGuy" explains how to stop incoming robocalls on your flip phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
